finding_results.notes: Lena's half now resolves into a public ownership argument around the patched swim and its cost, while Kai's half shifts into medical containment and a distinct public refusal of the 'technical failure' frame. The chapter no longer repeats the same ready-room to scoreboard to thin-handler aftermath pattern across both races.

Rebuilt the chapter with more sensory- and setting-led sentence openings, especially across the Kai section. Local sentence-start check on the revised chapter measured 30 'He' openers across 674 sentences (4.5%), below the 8% target.
Expanded the chapter to 7,924 words by adding causally necessary race detail, public consequence, medical/press aftermath, and the Lena-to-Kai connective pressure rather than filler.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_17.md:77; chapters/chapter_17.md:79; chapters/chapter_17.md:83; chapters/chapter_17.md:87; chapters/chapter_17.md:91; chapters/chapter_17.md:95; chapters/chapter_17.md:101; chapters/chapter_17.md:105; chapters/chapter_17.md:109; chapters/chapter_17.md:111; chapters/chapter_17.md:113
finding_results.notes: Replaced the thesis-delivering mixed-zone exchange (lines 77-121) with a messier press-scrum version. Key changes: (1) Reporter now interrupts himself to finish a slanted question about unpatched athletes competing (line 77/81), adding adversarial framing Lena must deflect. (2) Lena's answers are narrowings and refusals rather than philosophical positions — 'It is the result I have' (79), 'I don't know what other unpatched athletes can do. I swam my race.' (83), flat 'No' to expanding on no-regrets (87), clinical 'I didn't desynchronize. I was slow.' (91). (3) Cut the political/physical thesis definition entirely. (4) Cut 'I know more than the result' and the narrator commentary admiring its effect. (5) Replaced 'Because I was there' / 'For the race. All of it.' with 'I wanted to swim the race and know I was swimming it. That's all I can tell you.' (105) — phrased as a personal want with a stated limit, not a chapter thesis. (6) Handler cuts off a reporter mid-follow-up (111). (7) French reporter question updated to pick up the revised phrasing (113). Preserved: subtitle 'THIS WAS SLOWER. IT WAS ALSO MINE' (121), French-language answer about sequence (117), downstream references at lines 133 and 415. Lena's voice stays in her moderate-contraction, precise L2 register throughout.

Replaced the abstract cap on Lena's bronze aftermath with counted turns, replay image, and bodily residue; replaced the generic crowd-summary line with specific stadium behavior; replaced Kai's summary sentence about understanding with the exact quad and calf aftermath of the override.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 17 was structurally rebuilt to differentiate Lena's and Kai's public consequences, deepen the embodied cost of each race, and land the chapter's winning-versus-selfhood state shift while exceeding the required minimum length.

Single finding addressed. Lena's mixed-zone press exchange was reworked from a thesis-delivery sequence into a live-pressure scrum. Reporters now interrupt, push slanted framings, and recalibrate when Lena refuses to perform the narrative they want. Lena's answers became narrowings and deflections — plain facts and stated limits — rather than quotable philosophical positions. The political/physical definition speech and 'I know more than the result' were cut. The French reporter moment and subtitle caption are preserved intact. No other dialogue passages were altered; Kai's mixed-zone exchange and the Kai-Yusuf and Kai-Lena scenes were reviewed and found voice-consistent with no thesis-compression issues.
Chapter 17's flagged abstract capstones now land through concrete bodily or public detail while preserving Lena's slower-but-owned swim and Kai's manual override payoff.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=5, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
